political.s: MetLife Inc (parent company) made $746,814 in political contributions in 2024 and spent $1,420,000 on lobbying. The company maintains bipartisan political engagement through its PAC, contributing to candidates across party lines.

charity.s: MetLife Foundation has surpassed $1 billion in lifetime giving since 1976, with annual giving of approximately $37.6 million in 2023. The foundation focuses on economic inclusion, financial health, and resilient communities, supporting underserved populations globally.
charity.focus: Economic empowerment, financial health, resilient communities, career development, financial literacy

environment.s: MetLife is committed to Net Zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050, has planted trees as part of a 5 million tree pledge, and maintains 48% of global offices with green/healthy building certifications. The company reports declining GHG emissions from 2019-2024 and invests in renewable energy and sustainable forestry.

labor.s: MetLife provides comprehensive employee benefits including medical, dental, disability, 401(k) matching, pension, and paid time off. The company reports top quartile positioning on workforce diversity metrics and has established DEI initiatives. No major OSHA violations found in public data.

dei.s: MetLife committed over $2.5 billion to DEI initiatives by 2030, including $150 million in foundation funding for underserved communities. The company achieved recognition from Seramount for 4 consecutive years as a Leading Company on the Inclusion Index and maintains top quartile diversity positioning.

animals.s: MetLife Pet Insurance is a pet health insurance provider focused on supporting pet owners financially. No animal testing policies apply as the company provides insurance services rather than manufacturing products.

guns.s: MetLife is a financial services and insurance company with no involvement in firearm manufacturing or sales. Gun policy not applicable to business model.

privacy.s: MetLife maintains formal privacy policies for over 20 years, implements security measures including firewalls and encryption, and offers customer privacy rights including opt-out and data deletion requests. However, the company collects extensive personal and health data, shares information with affiliates, and uses tracking technologies including AI/ML for customer profiling.

execPay.s: CEO Michel Khalaf earned $20.32 million in total compensation (2024), comprised of $1.5M salary and $18.8M in bonuses/stock awards. Average employee compensation approximately $85K-$205K depending on role, suggesting CEO-to-worker pay ratio of approximately 100:1 or higher.
